data.description: JSON object containing the input data for the function

idempotencyKey.description: Optional idempotency key to handle duplicate requests within a given idempotency period.

isArchived.description: Updates the archived status of the environment. Set to true to archive the environment or false to unarchive it.

url.description: URL for the Inngest endpoint in the app

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

response.description: Optional inline JS function which is called to respond to GET requests
response.example: function respond(body, headers) { return { status: 200, headers: {}, body: JSON.parse(body)?.challenge } };

transform.description: The inline JS transform function for incoming events
transform.example: function transform(evt, headers = {}, queryParams = {}, raw = '') { return { name: 'webhook/request.received', data: evt }; }

events.description: Allow or deny specific events, e.g. 'orders/payment.created', or 'orders/*' for all orders. Only events in this list will be either allowed or denied.

code.description: Machine-readable code representing the type of diagnostic

context.description: Snippet of the query string around the position to provide context for the diagnostic

end.description: Ending character index in the query string where the diagnostic applies

start.description: Starting character index in the query string where the diagnostic applies

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the shape of this event's data, represented as a nested JSON object

name.description: Name of the column in the insights query result

values.description: Values for a single row of the insights query result, where each value corresponds to a column. The value is represented as a JSON object to allow for flexible typing (e.g., string, number, boolean, etc.)

description.description: Human-readable description of the table and its contents

name.description: Name of the table, used when writing queries

description.description: Human-readable description of the column and the data it contains

name.description: Name of the column in the insights table

type.description: Data type of the column (e.g., 'String', 'Int64', 'Array(String)', etc.)

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the function execution completed (if finished)

error.description: Error message if the function execution failed

queuedAt.description: Timestamp when the function execution was enqueued

result.description: JSON string containing the function result (only available for completed synchronous invocations)

runId.description: Unique identifier for this function execution

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the function execution started (if started)

data.description: Paginated list of event type schemas
List of available tables that can be queried via the Insights query endpoint

columns.description: Column metadata for the insights query result

diagnostics.description: Any non-fatal diagnostics related to the insights query execution

rows.description: Rows of the insights query result, where each row contains a list of values corresponding to the columns

sql.example: SELECT function_id, COUNT(*) AS total, countIf(status = 'Failed') AS failures, failures / total AS failure_rate FROM function_runs WHERE started_at >= now() - INTERVAL 7 DAY GROUP BY function_id ORDER BY failure_rate DESC LIMIT 10

summary.description: A natural language summary of the generated query, useful for explaining the query's intent to agents
summary.example: This query retrieves the top 10 functions with the highest failure rate over the past week, showing the total number of runs, number of failures, and calculated failure rate for each function.

prompt.description: Natural language description of the query to generate
prompt.example: Show me the top 10 functions by failure rate in the last 7 days

query.description: The insights query to execute, written in modified ClickHouse SQL

info.description: The v2 API delivers a significantly improved developer experience with consistent design patterns and enhanced functionality.
info.title: Inngest REST API v2

get.description: Returns the account for the authenticated user
Fetches function configuration and status details for a function within an app

post.description: Invokes a function, executing the function either asynchronously or synchronously based on the mode parameter in the request body

parameters.description: The ID of the app containing the function
parameters.name: appId

parameters.description: The ID of the function to invoke
parameters.name: functionId

202.description: Function invoked synchronously but has not yet finished executing

409.description: Conflict - this function has already been invoked using the given idempotency key.  Note that this returns the previously invoked run's data.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - function was rate limited, debounced, skipped, or otherwise could not be invoked

429.description: Too Many Requests - API rate limit exceeded.  The request was entirely rejected, and no function was invoked.

post.description: Sync an app at the provided URL.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - sync failed. The response body is still SyncAppResponse and includes the persisted deploy plus a coded error under data.latestSync.error.

get.description: Lists all webhooks in the specified environment

parameters.description: Number of webhooks to return per page (min: 1, max: 100)
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Creates a new webhook for receiving events

409.description: Conflict - webhook with same URL already exists

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - business logic validation failed

parameters.description: Number of environments to return per page (min: 1, max: 250)
parameters.name: limit

patch.description: Partially updates an environment. Only the provided fields will be modified.

parameters.description: The ID of the environment to update
parameters.name: id

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - business logic validation failed

get.description: Lists function runs triggered by a specific event

parameters.description: Number of runs to return per page (min: 1, max: 40)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Lists event keys for a specific account, optionally filtered by environment. If the environment header isn't specified, this returns the default production environment's keys.

parameters.description: Number of event keys to return per page (min: 1, max: 100)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Lists signing keys for a specific account, optionally filtered by environment.  If the environment header isn't specified, this returns the default production environment's keys.

parameters.description: Number of signing keys to return per page (min: 1, max: 100)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Lists sub-accounts (if you have partner access)

parameters.description: Number of accounts to return per page (min: 1, max: 1000)
parameters.name: limit

errors.message: You do not have access to the partner API.  Please reach out to sales@inngest.com to gain access.
You do not have access to the partner API.  Please reach out to sales@inngest.com to gain access.

post.description: Creates a sub-account (if you have partner access)

errors.message: You do not have access to the partner API.  Please reach out to sales@inngest.com to gain access.
You do not have access to the partner API.  Please reach out to sales@inngest.com to gain access.

get.description: Fetches the canonical run summary for a single function run
Fetches the trace tree for a single function run
